subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical progression from introduction through data analysis to conclusion
Well-structured report with headings, tables, diagrams and appendix aiding navigation
Mostly concise, with relevant content focused on testing normality and advert length implications
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and redundant explanations reduce conciseness
Minor formatting issues in appendix and long narrative paragraphs
Some diagram axes lack numeric labels and units
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-3 performance: The exploration is coherent and logically developed with clear structure, and most content is relevant. Minor digressions and formatting weaknesses prevent it from reaching the exceptional clarity and conciseness required for band-4.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of Î¼, Ï, PDF notation and interval notation
Effective integration of multiple representations (tables, charts, overlaid PDF)
Good logical flow and clarity of mathematical explanations
weaknesses: Minor notation inconsistencies (duplicate â symbols, bolding)
Occasional mis-phrasing (e.g. âhigher arrivals around the meanâ)
Some redundant derivations and missed opportunities to consolidate equations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Elevated to band-4: While not flawless, the studentâs notation is largely consistent, representations are used effectively, and explanations are clear throughout. This warrants the top mark for Mathematical Communication in light of the overall strength and minor nature of remaining issues.

strengths: Well-described primary data collection showing independent initiative
Personal anecdote effectively motivates the investigation
Decisions on interval width and scaling show creativity
weaknesses: Methodology follows standard textbook examples rather than fully original pathways
Personal approach is clear but not deeply innovative
Creativity is solid but not sustained at an exceptional level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-2 performance: Significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach are evident, but the work does not reach the highest level of innovation or originality needed for band-3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on audience behaviour and limitations of sample size throughout
Thoughtful analysis of results and process with practical implications
Clear evaluation of outcomes and recommendations for future data collection
weaknesses: Depth of reflection is moderate; broader methodological implications are not fully explored
Limited interrogation of normality assumptions and grouping choices
Future recommendations lack rigorous quantitative framing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-2 performance: The reflection is meaningful and sustained, with thoughtful analysis and clear evaluation, but it does not exhibit the depth or critical insight required for the highest band.

strengths: Relevant mathematics is used effectively in a coherent chain from raw data to inverse-normal probabilities
Techniques are fully commensurate with SL level and correctly applied in context
Demonstrates thorough understanding and mostly correct application of statistical formulas and PDF scaling
weaknesses: Justification for chosen interval width is absent
Key steps (z-score standardisation, rounding consistency) are sometimes omitted or inconsistent
Minor interpretative mis-statements and percentile choice mismatch require clarification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Raised to band-5: Mathematics is applied comprehensively at SL level with solid understanding and accuracy. Although some procedural justifications and consistency issues remain, the overall depth and correctness of mathematical work merit a higher mark than band-4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations generally proceed step-by-step (data â statistics â model â probabilities) and the reader can follow the reasoning.  Occasional wording issues (e.g. âhigher arrivals around the meanâ mis-phrasing) slightly reduce clarity, but overall logical structure and communication are good.

Calculations of Î¼, Ï, probabilities and inverse normals are accurate, and the PDF scaling is mathematically sound even if approximate.  Minor interpretative mis-statements (e.g. over-interpretation of empirical rule percentages) do not undermine overall correctness, indicating good understanding with mostly correct work, hence band 4.

The investigation makes effective use of several representations: raw time-stamp table, grouped-frequency table, bar chart, overlaid PDF curve and probability statements.  Each representation is appropriately linked to the text.  A cumulative frequency graph or boxplot would have strengthened the set, so the use is âeffectiveâ rather than âhighly effectiveâ.

The exploration progresses smoothly from a personally-motivated introduction, through data collection, modelling and probability work, to a conclusion that answers the guiding questions.  Transitions between sections are generally clear and each idea builds on the previous one (e.g. descriptive statistics â empirical rule test â PDF fitting â probability calculations).  A few digressions (re-stating definitions, repeating values) prevent the argument from being exemplary, but overall logical development is consistently evident.

Designing the data-collection method, deciding on interval width and scaling the PDF all show creativity and initiative beyond a purely textbook exercise.  These aspects are good but not exceptional or sustained throughout, justifying the middle band.

The empirical-rule comparison and the discussion of advert-length scenarios constitute thoughtful analysis of both results and process.  Yet the critique is not especially deep: assumptions of normality and grouping effects are acknowledged but not rigorously interrogated, fitting the âthoughtfulâ rather than âdeep and insightfulâ description.

Outcomes are evaluated (10-minute adverts judged appropriate; suggestions for larger sample, different genres).  The student draws clear implications but does not extend to comprehensive future planning or alternative modelling, placing the work in the âclear evaluationâ band.

The student reflects on what the probabilities mean for audience behaviour and notes sample-size and genre limitations.  Reflection appears at several points, not just the conclusion, making it âmeaningful throughoutâ.  Still, the depth is moderate; broader implications or alternative models are not examined, so it does not reach the highest band.

The student employs standard notation for Î¼, Ï, PDF, P( a < X < b ) and uses interval notation correctly.  Minor slips occur (duplicate â symbols, inconsistent bolding of variables), but symbols are used in a mostly consistent and correct manner across the exploration, satisfying the âmostly consistent useâ descriptor.

The exploration is clearly anchored in the studentâs personal cinema experiences and decisions (choice of film, timing intervals), demonstrating a personal approach.  The approach is evident but not deeply innovative, placing it comfortably in the âclear evidenceâ band.

Almost all material is relevant to determining whether arrival times are normally distributed and what this means for advert length.  Redundant explanations of basic terms and the repeated calculation of the amplitude show that the writing could be more concise, yet superfluous content is limited.  Therefore the work is âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ rather than âhighly concise with all content relevantâ.

All mathematics used (grouped data, mean, standard deviation, PDF, scaling factor, normal probabilities, inverse normal) is directly relevant to answering the research question and is applied in a coherent chain.  The mathematics is not exhaustive but is used effectively across the exploration, warranting band 4.

Collecting primary data in person and deciding to model it with a normal distribution show significant independent thinking.  However, the mathematical pathway (empirical rule, PDF, basic probabilities) closely follows textbook examples, so the evidence is strong but not outstanding.

Headings, numbered tables, labelled diagrams and an appendix give the report a clear structure (introduction, data representation, analysis, conclusion).  The reader can locate information quickly and follow the investigation without difficulty.  Minor weaknesses (e.g. some long narrative paragraphs, appendix formatting issues) stop the organisation short of the âexceptionally well-organisedâ descriptor, but the overall layout is clearly âwell organised with clear structureâ.

The techniques are solidly within the SL syllabus (statistics and probability) and are applied correctly in context.  There is no extension beyond the course, but the work is fully commensurate with SL level, justifying band 4.

annotations.comment: The aim is clearly stated, but specifying precise mathematical objectives (e.g. parameters to estimate) would strengthen focus.
The methodology description, including timeâstamping arrivals, is detailed and demonstrates clear independent initiative.
The studentâs personal anecdote provides strong engagement and effectively motivates the investigation.
The series of questions in this paragraph is insightful but repetitive; condense them to improve clarity and focus.
The choice of three-minute intervals lacks justification. Discuss how this bin width affects the smoothness and fit of your distribution.
Consider presenting a concise grouped-frequency table showing only interval midpoints and counts to enhance readability.
Notation in Table 2 (overlines, bold zeros) is inconsistent. Standardise formatting to avoid confusion.
Table 2 effectively summarises arrivals after the scheduled start, giving clear insight into audience behaviour.
You exclude pre-13:45 data from Table 2; explicitly note the rationale to clarify why early intervals were omitted.
Diagram 1 is well-labelled and integrates smoothly with the text, aiding interpretation of frequency patterns.
The axes on Diagram 1 lack numeric labels and units; adding these would improve clarity and precision.
When introducing the PDF, explicitly connect Î¼ and Ï to the computed values to link theory with data.
The LaTeX presentation of $$\,$$ the mean and standard deviation is precise and aids readability.
Rounding of Î¼ and Ï is inconsistent; decide on a uniform level of precision and apply throughout.
To check the empirical rule, include the standardisation steps (z-scores) for each interval to reinforce mathematical justification.
Interpreting 71.43% >68% as more peaked demands discussion of kurtosis or sample variability; deepen this reflection.
Presenting PDF values as a table is thorough, but overlaying these on the histogram would enhance visual comparison.
The substitution into $$f(x)=	frac1{5.62oot{2 inom{}}}e^{-	frac12 igl(	frac{x+0.905}{5.62} igr)^2}$$ is executed correctly, demonstrating solid mathematical application.
The note that y-values were ânot visibleâ lacks detail on visibility threshold; explain how scaling decisions were quantified.
The amplitude calculation is well explained; relating the stretch factor back to the maximum frequency anchors the model to data.
Equation for A is repeated in two blocks; consolidate into a single derivation to eliminate redundancy.
Diagram 2 successfully aligns the scaled PDF curve with observed frequencies, reinforcing the normal model visually.
The probability calculation P(â12<x<0) should include the conversion to z-scores; this step is key for mathematical clarity.
The interpretation that almost half the audience arrived early links results to behaviour well, adding real-world insight.
For inverse normal results, overlay percentile thresholds on the PDF/histogram to make the choice of advert lengths more concrete visually.
Thereâs an inconsistency: text states 85% threshold but calculation uses 80%. Clarify percentile choice.
The conclusion synthesises statistical findings and practical recommendations coherently, giving a strong narrative closure.
The recommendation for larger sample sizes is sound; consider quantifying its impact on confidence intervals or margin of error.
The conclusion overlooks potential bias from focusing on a single childrenâs film; adding nuance would demonstrate critical reflection.
